c25.q: What year did the publisher of Labyrinth end?
c25.gen_cached:
The publisher of *Labyrinth* (the game by Acornsoft) did not "end" in a specific year, but the company responsible for publishing it, **Acornsoft**, ceased to operate as a separate entity in **January 1986**, when David Johnson-Davies, its managing director, departed.

After that, Acornsoft was no longer an independent publisher. However, the **Acornsoft name** continued to be used by **Superior Software**, which was granted a license to publish and re-release many Acornsoft games, including *Labyrinth*, in compilations like *Acornsoft Hits*.

So, while the original publisher (Acornsoft) effectively ended operations in **1986**, the game *Labyrinth* was later re-released by Superior Software, meaning the title continued to be available beyond that year.
